example_sentence: Creo que deberíamos hablar con el director.
example_translation: I think we should talk to the director.

example_sentence: No estoy de acuerdo con esa decisión.
example_translation: I don't agree with that decision.

example_sentence: Es una buena idea; sin embargo, necesitamos más tiempo.
example_translation: It's a good idea; however, we need more time.

example_sentence: Es caro y, además, está muy lejos.
example_translation: It's expensive and, furthermore, it's very far.

translation: I mean / that is / like (Mexican filler)
example_sentence: O sea, no entiendo por qué lo hiciste así.
example_translation: I mean, I don't understand why you did it that way.

example_sentence: De hecho, ya lo intentamos antes y no funcionó.
example_translation: In fact, we already tried it before and it didn't work.

example_sentence: El plan es bueno; por otro lado, es muy costoso.
example_translation: The plan is good; on the other hand, it's very costly.

example_sentence: Con todo respeto, creo que hay un error en ese dato.
example_translation: With all due respect, I think there's an error in that data.

translation: at the end of the day / when all is said and done
example_sentence: A fin de cuentas, lo que importa es que todos estén bien.
example_translation: At the end of the day, what matters is that everyone is okay.

translation: what do you think? / what's your opinion?
example_translation: What do you think about the new government?

translation: well, I don't know (Mexican polite disagreement signal)
example_sentence: — ¿Te parece buena idea? — Pues, no sé...
example_translation: — Does it seem like a good idea to you? — Well, I don't know...
